description: First wave for SBOS-A1-ERP: turn the empty placeholder repo into a runnable foundation that mirrors the A1-ERP-HY dmux pattern and seeds the first domain module (RBAC) from there.
launcherCommand: bash -lc 'echo "[worker] {task_file} ready in {worktree_path}"; echo "--- handoff: {handoff_file} ---"; echo "--- status: {status_file} ---"; echo "--- source of truth: ~/dev/A1-ERP-HY (read-only reference) ---"; sleep 86400'

workers.name: repo-foundation
workers.task:
Bootstrap the SBOS-A1-ERP repository with a minimal but real Node.js + TypeScript foundation so subsequent waves can `npm install` and run `node --test`.

Scope (commit each item):
1. `package.json` — name `sbos-a1-erp`, type `module`, engines `node >=20`, scripts: `test` (`node --test --test-concurrency=4 --test-timeout=60000`), `lint` (`eslint .`), `format` (`prettier --check .`). Dev deps only at this stage; do NOT pull runtime libs yet.
2. `.nvmrc` — `20`.
3. `.gitignore` — node_modules, .DS_Store, *.log, .claude/worktrees/, .orchestration/*/[a-z]*/, !.orchestration/README.md, coverage/, dist/.
4. `AGENTS.md` — agent conventions: TDD (test first, then implementation), 80% coverage floor, immutable data, conventional commits, no hardcoded secrets, prefer porting proven code from `~/dev/A1-ERP-HY` over net-new invention.
5. `tsconfig.json` — strict, target ES2022, module NodeNext, moduleResolution NodeNext, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true`, outDir `dist`.
6. `eslint.config.js` — flat config: `@eslint/js` recommended, `typescript-eslint` strict-type-checked, no console.log (warn), no-floating-promises (error).
7. `.prettierrc.json` — singleQuote true, semi true, trailingComma 'all', printWidth 100.
8. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — node 20 matrix, runs `npm ci`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, `npm run format:check`.
9. Sanity test: `test/sanity.test.js` — a single `node:test` that asserts `1 + 1 === 2` and `process.version.startsWith('v20')`. Confirms the runner works.

Run `node --test test/sanity.test.js` after each major step. Final full check: `npm test` must pass with at least 1/1. Commit with `chore: bootstrap sbos-a1-erp repo foundation` and write handoff.md listing every file created, the final test count, and any deviations from the plan.

Read-only reference: `~/dev/A1-ERP-HY/package.json` and `~/dev/A1-ERP-HY/HANDOFF.md` for style hints — DO NOT copy runtime code, only conventions.

workers.name: seed-from-a1-erp-hy
workers.task:
Seed SBOS-A1-ERP with the canonical design docs from A1-ERP-HY so every future worker has the same north star. This is documentation seeding only — no code ports here.

Mirror these files (read from `~/dev/A1-ERP-HY/`, write to SBOS-A1-ERP with the same relative path, and prepend a one-line `<!-- Mirrored from A1-ERP-HY @ <sha> on <date> -->` note):
- `docs/ERP_COMPARISON_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`
- `docs/RBAC_SYSTEM.md`
- `docs/DMUX_WORKFLOWS.md`
- `docs/PROJECT_STATUS.md`
- `HANDOFF.md` (only the high-level architecture summary, not the full 172KB — extract the first 400 lines: armosphera one claude description, 9 UI domains list, hardening-slice summary, build & test instructions). Store as `docs/HANDOFF-SUMMARY.md` and link to the A1-ERP-HY source.

For each mirrored file, add a footer section `## Provenance` listing the source path, the commit SHA you read from, and the mirroring date.

Then write `docs/SBOS_VS_A1_ERP_HY.md` (new, ~60 lines) describing:
- What SBOS-A1-ERP is: a fresh, clean-slate repo that will house the Armosphera One Claude public release, separate from the private A1-ERP-HY development repo.
- The relationship: SBOS-A1-ERP is the public-facing open-core; A1-ERP-HY remains the private R&D / hardening ground. Code ports go A1-ERP-HY → SBOS-A1-ERP after de-private-ation (secrets scrubbed, vendor names normalized).
- What is OUT of scope for SBOS-A1-ERP initially: tenant/operational hardening, paid-integration keys, anything brand-specific.
- Initial module priority: RBAC (domain-agnostic), then i18n (en, hy-Am, ru), then Armenia-specific tax (VAT form, chart of accounts, e-invoice). See A1-ERP-HY `armeniaChartOfAccounts.js`, `vatReturn.js`, `einvoice.js` for the canonical implementations.

Commit with `docs: seed canonical docs from a1-erp-hy` and handoff.md must list every file added with size and SHA1 of the source blob.

workers.name: rbac-port
workers.task:
Port the RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) subsystem from A1-ERP-HY into SBOS-A1-ERP as the first domain-agnostic module. RBAC has no Armenia-specific logic so it's a clean first port.

Source directory: `~/dev/A1-ERP-HY/server/rbac/` (8 files: `permissions.js`, `roles.js`, `matrix.js`, `roleMatrix.js`, `guards.js`, `schema.sql`, `seed.js`, `routes.js`, `index.js`).
Source test: `~/dev/A1-ERP-HY/test/rbac.test.js` (45/45 passing).

Target directory: `server/rbac/`. Copy files with these modifications:
1. **Strip all brand/internal identifiers.** Search the source for any string matching `a1-erp-hy`, `armosphera`, `samvel`, `HayHashvapah`, internal tenant ids, and either remove or replace with `sbos-a1-erp`. Use a single-pass regex pass and verify with `git grep -nE 'armosphera|hayhashvapah|samvel' server/rbac/` returning zero matches.
2. **Convert the RBAC module to be framework-agnostic.** A1-ERP-HY ties guards to its Express middleware. In SBOS-A1-ERP, expose `requirePerm(permKey, ctx) => boolean` and `requireRole(roleName, ctx) => boolean` as pure functions. The Express adapter becomes a separate `server/rbac/express-adapter.js` (write a thin wrapper). Tests must use the pure functions, not Express.
3. **Permissions catalog** stays at 300+ keys / 18 categories, but add the same additional keys the A1-ERP-HY rbac-catalog worker added: `mfg.*`, `mrkt.*`, `compliance.*`, `ai.agent.*`, `tenant.*` (10 new keys total, with tests for each).
4. **Schema** (`schema.sql`) — keep Postgres-compatible but rename the schema from `a1_rbac` to `sbos_rbac`. Provide a `server/rbac/migrations/0001_init.sql` with the same DDL.
5. **Tests** must mirror the structure of `~/dev/A1-ERP-HY/test/rbac.test.js` (45 tests) PLUS the 10 new keys = 55 tests minimum. Run with `node --test server/rbac/**/*.test.js`.

Hardening requirements (from A1-ERP-HY's 51+ hardening slices — apply the relevant ones):
- `requirePerm` must short-circuit and return `false` on missing context (no throws in the permission check path).
- `requireRole` must respect the role hierarchy (Owner ⊇ Admin ⊇ …) and `session.impersonation` context.
- No `eval`, no `Function()` constructor, no `new Function(...)` anywhere in the module.
- All SQL must be parameterized (`$1`, `$2`, …). No string concatenation in queries.
- Sensitive permission keys (`ai.agent.*`, `tenant.*`, `compliance.*`) must require MFA — encode this in the guards via a `requiresMfa(permKey)` helper.

Acceptance criteria:
- `npm test` passes 55/55.
- `git grep -nE 'armosphera|hayhashvapah|samvel|a1-erp-hy' server/rbac/` returns zero matches.
- `git grep -nE 'eval\(|new Function' server/rbac/` returns zero matches.
- `git grep -nE 'SELECT.*\+ |INSERT.*\+ |UPDATE.*\+ |DELETE.*\+ ' server/rbac/` returns zero matches (string-concat SQL).

Commit with `feat(rbac): port rbac subsystem from a1-erp-hy` and handoff.md must show the test output and the four grep commands' empty results.

workers.name: dmux-docs
workers.task:
Write the SBOS-A1-ERP-specific dmux-workflows and project-status documentation. The dmux-workflows pattern is already wired (scripts/orchestrate-worktrees.js exists from this bootstrap commit), but SBOS-A1-ERP needs its own guide tuned to its reality (empty-repo cold start, A1-ERP-HY as source of truth, no runtime yet).

Write these files:
1. `docs/DMUX_WORKFLOWS.md` (~200 lines) —
   - Section 1: What is dmux, and what is the SBOS-A1-ERP flavor of it (we use a tmux + node helper, not the standalone `dmux` binary — explain why)
   - Section 2: plan.json schema with a real SBOS-A1-ERP example (link to `.orchestration/sbos-a1-erp-bootstrap.json`)
   - Section 3: placeholders table (`{worktree_path}`, `{task_file}`, `{handoff_file}`, `{status_file}`, `{repo_root}`)
   - Section 4: seedPaths contract and why we seed the orchestrator itself in wave 0
   - Section 5: how to run (`node scripts/orchestrate-worktrees.js .orchestration/<plan>.json [--dry-run|--execute|--no-tmux]`)
   - Section 6: worker protocol (read task.md, commit, write handoff.md, tick status.md)
   - Section 7: A1-ERP-HY ↔ SBOS-A1-ERP worker convention: workers in SBOS-A1-ERP treat A1-ERP-HY as **read-only reference** — port with provenance
   - Section 8: troubleshooting (pane not responding, merge conflicts, high token usage, tmux not found)
   - Section 9: example wave 0 invocation
2. `docs/PROJECT_STATUS.md` (~100 lines) —
   - Current state: wave 0 in progress (4 bootstrap workers)
   - Architecture: SBOS-A1-ERP (public open-core) vs A1-ERP-HY (private R&D)
   - Module pipeline: RBAC (port) → i18n (en/hy-Am/ru) → Armenia tax (VAT, chart of accounts, e-invoice) → CRM → Finance
   - Open questions: package manager (npm vs pnpm), runtime (Node only vs Bun compat), CI provider (GitHub Actions vs other)
   - Test gate: 55+ tests after RBAC port, 80%+ coverage floor per `common/testing.md`
   - How to run: `nvm use && npm install && npm test`
3. `docs/AGENT_BRIEF.md` (~50 lines) — one-page brief for any new agent/human joining the project: goal, current wave, where to read first, conventions, who to ask.
4. `AGENTS.md` is owned by the `repo-foundation` worker — coordinate via status.md, do NOT duplicate content.

Commit with `docs: dmux-workflows and project-status guides` and handoff.md must include a TOC of all three new docs with line counts and a one-line summary of each section.
